specification.description:
Feature Specification: base-map-selection
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.description: Add a dropdown to the control panel allowing the user to select between 'Satellite + Roads (Esri)', 'Street Map — Liberty', and 'Street Map — Bright'. When switched, tear down and rebuild the map state retaining the camera viewport.

specification.description:
Feature Specification: control-panel-ui
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.description: Provide a floating control panel that displays: the user's UUID, current live longitude, latitude, and altitude. Also provide interactive buttons to 'Reset Default View' and 'Save State' (manually override/save cookie state).

specification.description:
Feature Specification: custom-data-overlay
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.description: Add an 'Overlay' section to the control panel where users can paste a custom map URL (XYZ, pmtiles://, or cog://), toggle its visibility on/off via a switch, and adjust its transparency using a 0-100% opacity slider.

specification.description:
DATA-MODEL: globe-viewer-state FIELDS:
- longitude: <class 'float'>
- longitude_note: <class 'str'>
- latitude: <class 'float'>
- latitude_note: <class 'str'>
- altitude_m: <class 'float'>
- altitude_m_note: <class 'str'>
- base_map: <class 'str'>
specification.notes: A data class to specify what data fields will be stored for remembering cookied user's current state within the app, during and between sessions.

context.altitude_m_note: The user's portal altitude from earth mean surface level
context.latitude_note: The user's view portal's center point latitude in decimal form
context.longitude_note: The user's view portal's center point longitude in decimal form

specification.description:
Requirement TITLE: globe-projection REQUIREMENT-ID: RQ5
Insert REQUIREMENT-ID into any source code for cross reference purposes.
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.action: Use MapLibre GL JS >= 5.0.0 and call map.setProjection({type:'globe'}) inside the map load event.
context.benefit: Provides a 3D globe visualization instead of a flat 2D map.
context.title: globe-projection

specification.description:
Feature Specification: on-first-visit
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.description: 1. When a user visits for the very first time, generate a cookie called GLOBAL_VIEWER_UUID (containing a UUID) to know that they've visited. Generate a second cookie named GLOBAL_VIEWER_STATE to contain GlobalViewerPortalState's values, stored as JSON that's been URI-encoded; LLM will provide code that performs the encodeURIComponent(JSON.stringify(GlobalViewerPortalState instance)) and cookie decoding e.g. JSON.parse(decodeURIComponent(cookie value)). 2. Perform the steps in OnVisit(Feature) to intialize the portal display.

specification.description:
Feature Specification: on-return-visit
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.description: 1. Check for GLOBAL_VIEWER_UUID, and if GLOBAL_VIEWER_STATE restore its encoded values to the GlobalViewerPortalState instance. 2. Perform the steps in OnVisit(Feature) to intialize the portal display

specification.description:
Feature Specification: on-visit
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.description: When a user opens the site in a web browser, perform all software tasks necessary to load MapLibre GL JS's libraries, set the map/globe view portal up, initialize the portal to the default longitude, latitude and altitude

specification.description:
Feature Specification: on-visit-close
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.description: 1. Ensure that when the current browser tab or window closes, that the current GlobalViewerPortalState values are written to GLOBAL_VIEWER_STATE one last time. 2. Then perform all steps necessary to shut down MapLibre GL JS's currently running code, unload allocated objects -whatever's recommended by that project.

specification.description:
Requirement TITLE: one-source-html-file REQUIREMENT-ID: RQ1
Insert REQUIREMENT-ID into any source code for cross reference purposes.
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.action: The LLM should generate all the source in a single HTML file, including ECMAscript
context.benefit: Simplify this project at its early stages, while still evaluating libspec and MapLibre GL JS
context.title: one-source-html-file

specification.description:
Requirement TITLE: specify-numbered-output-filenames REQUIREMENT-ID: RQ4
Insert REQUIREMENT-ID into any source code for cross reference purposes.
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.action: When writing generated index.html, use the format index%03d.html and increment the number each time, so that previous versions are preserved.
context.benefit: Ensure this web app runs when using CDN-loaded resources
context.title: specify-numbered-output-filenames

specification.description:
Feature Specification: status-bar-ui
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.description: Provide a floating status bar in the bottom-left corner to show portal loading logs, errors, and interaction status messages.

specification.description:
Requirement TITLE: support-cog REQUIREMENT-ID: RQ7
Insert REQUIREMENT-ID into any source code for cross reference purposes.
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.action: Load geotiff.js via CDN and implement a custom 'cog://' protocol handler that downloads, normalizes, and color-maps GeoTIFF windows on-the-fly to XYZ canvas blobs.
context.benefit: Enables direct-in-browser rendering of massive remote sensing raster files without a backend server.
context.title: support-cog

specification.description:
Requirement TITLE: support-pmtiles REQUIREMENT-ID: RQ6
Insert REQUIREMENT-ID into any source code for cross reference purposes.
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.action: Load the pmtiles.js library via CDN and register the 'pmtiles://' protocol with maplibregl.
context.benefit: Allows rendering single-file PMTiles map tile archives.
context.title: support-pmtiles

specification.description:
Requirement TITLE: target-compatible-ecmascript REQUIREMENT-ID: RQ2
Insert REQUIREMENT-ID into any source code for cross reference purposes.
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.action: The LLM should generate code compatible with modern browsers, and use Babel & core-js to ensure as wide of an audience as possible via transpiling and/or polyfilling
context.benefit: Ensure this web app runs on as many devices as possible
context.title: target-compatible-ecmascript

specification.description:
Requirement TITLE: Make use of MapeLibre GL JS open-source library REQUIREMENT-ID: RQ0
Insert REQUIREMENT-ID into any source code for cross reference purposes.
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.action: provides core map and globe visualization software and tile data
context.benefit: provides a software base that handles an estimated 99% of this app's needs
context.title: Make use of MapeLibre GL JS open-source library

specification.description:
Requirement TITLE: specify-mimetype-for-cdn-scripts REQUIREMENT-ID: RQ3
Insert REQUIREMENT-ID into any source code for cross reference purposes.
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.action: When generating <Script> tags for CDN resources, the LLM should generate <script> tags that specify mime-types, otherwise browser error handlers will throw errors and prevent the page from rendering.
context.benefit: Ensure this web app runs when using CDN-loaded resources
context.title: specify-mimetype-for-cdn-scripts

specification.description:
Feature Specification: watermark-branding
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.

context.description: Add translucent 'THUCYDIDES AEROSPACE' watermark bars locked to the top and bottom of the app layout window.
